title: What I think about Florida, Tampa, Miami   Request

enrichment.summary: Kyle responds to a video request about Florida cities, sharing his limited personal experience visiting Miami and Epcot as a child. He discusses Miami's energy, latitude mysticism, the Coral Castle, the city's crime history tied to the 1980s Cuban exodus and cocaine trade, and expresses curiosity about Tampa while noting he has not visited it.
enrichment.speaker: kyle

stances.position: Cool, magical vibe worth visiting but has a crime legacy from the 1980s Cuban criminal exodus and cocaine era
Impressive and mysterious — the builder figured out something about levitating rocks that nobody can explain
Strongly against it — anacondas and saltwater crocodiles should be eliminated; native small alligators are fine
Would be a top destination if living in a van due to warm weather
Believes 19.5 degrees latitude is a special energy vortex shared by Miami, Hawaii, Los Angeles, and the Giza pyramids

transcript.text: Okay, hello, video request.
What do you think of Florida, especially the cities of Miami and Fort Lauderdale and Tampa and St. Petersburg?
I haven't traveled through Florida very much.
I have spent a couple days in Miami.
I drove down, borrowed a friend's car, and drove down from the north part of Miami down to Key West and back.
And, yeah, it had a cool energy about it.
It was a cool vibe, like it was a magical place.
Miami is right at the 19.5 degree latitude, a magical latitude of energy coming into the sphere from the cosmos.
It was Lake Hawaii and Los Angeles and the pyramids of Kiza.
Inside of Miami is that place, the Coral Castle.
I checked that out.
That was pretty cool.
The dude figured out how to levitate rocks to create his own castle out of giant coral roadblocks.
Nobody knows how he did it.
A big wall and then his own house with giant stones stacked on top of each other.
Pretty cool.
And then I saw the bayou.
Some, like, preserve with a bunch of crocodiles everywhere.
Yeah.
And the downtown area, the party scene looked pretty cool.
All these bars, jam-packed with people, lots of people out in the street at night selling stuff right on the beach.
Yeah, that place was really cool to visit, pass through.
And the beach looked nice.
The water's warm.
Nice and, you know, shallow as you go out.
But that's all that I know of Florida.
Florida, the weather is really nice.
I went down there and I was in 8th grade to check out Epcot Center.
And the weather is just so warm and humid and those big, thick blades of grass down there.
It's cool to go to a different place from Colorado.
We have a totally different nature.
Yeah, I heard that, like, people aren't always the nicest in Miami.
But, yeah, I think we've really been dealing with lots of crime after Cuba kicked all the criminals out in, like, 1981.
And then they started, that was a big cocaine center in the 80s with a lot of violence and stuff.
So, they got a lot of movies and stuff.
Miami Vice is based out of Miami.
And, yeah, it's a cool little vortex of energy.
A big city down on the southeastern corner of America.
You know, northwestern is Seattle, southwestern Los Angeles.
Miami's got southeastern.
Yeah, but I haven't been through these other places.
I've looked at, I know where Tampa is.
I've seen it on a map.
And that looks like a cool place right next to the water.
Lots of little inlets and stuff.
But I think it's, like, really wild.
So, a lot of snakes and stuff.
It's too bad people are dropping off their anacondas in Florida.
They didn't have these giant man-eating monsters out there.
We should kill that stuff off.
And I don't want any Austrian crocodiles, saltwater crocodiles, over there either.
These little ones, little American-sized ones are cute.
They're fine.
They're good enough.
But, yeah, but I haven't been there.
I thought, you know, if I was living in my van, that would definitely be one of the first places I'd want to go.
It's for the winter, for sure.
Florida, man.
Maybe one day.
Okay, so that's what I think about Florida.
Peace out.
